title: Taxes
lyrics:
[Verse 1]
I should burn in hell
  > [#1, 35 votes] Winter (or at least the character on this album) has made many bad decisions in his life. He’s abandoned his family, done cocaine, bowed down to authority, and contributed to corrupt society....
I should burn in hell
But I don't deserve this
Nobody deserves this
Ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh

[Verse 2]
If you want me to pay my taxes
  > [#2, 21 votes] Taxes are compulsory by nature. They are not freely offered but demanded. The “nail me down” line indicates the narrator would sooner sacrifice their life than pay up. The song’s closing reference to...
If you want me to pay my taxes
You'd better come over with a crucifix
  > [#3, 20 votes] The lyric “You’d better come over with a crucifix / You’re gonna have to nail me down” is a reference to crucifixion. Given the following verse’s “Doctor, heal yourself,” the passage below, spoken to...
You're gonna have to nail me down
Ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh-ooh

[Verse 3]
Doctor, doctor, heal yourself
  > [#4, 54 votes] From the ancient proverb dating back to the 6th Century BCE (“physician, heal thyself”), it refers to a healer’s inability to cure themselves, with the moral being that one should fix one’s problems...
Doctor, doctor, heal yourself
And I will break my own heart
I will break my own heart from now on
Ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh-ooh, ooh-ooh
Ooh
